fileContext.overview: Manages a llama-server child process, providing a singleton instance for starting, stopping, and monitoring the server.

symbols.name: ServerManager
symbols.purpose: Singleton class for managing a llama-server child process.
symbols.behavior: Provides methods for starting, stopping, and monitoring the server, as well as emitting events to registered callbacks.
symbols.limitations: Only one instance of the server can run at a time.

symbols.name: start
symbols.purpose: Starts the llama-server child process with the given options.
symbols.behavior: Checks if the server is already running, stops it if necessary, and then starts the new server with the provided options.
symbols.limitations: Fails if the model file or llama-server binary is not found.

symbols.name: stop
symbols.behavior: Kills the server process and emits a 'stopped' event to registered callbacks.
symbols.limitations: Fails if the server process is not found.

symbols.name: dispose
symbols.purpose: Disposes of the llama-server child process and clears any registered callbacks.
symbols.behavior: Kills the server process and clears any registered callbacks.

symbols.name: pollHealth
symbols.purpose: Polls the server's health endpoint to determine its status.
symbols.behavior: Sends a GET request to the server's health endpoint and updates the server's status accordingly.
symbols.limitations: Fails if the server is not running or the health endpoint is not available.

symbols.name: addLog
symbols.purpose: Adds a log line to the server's log buffer.
symbols.behavior: Pushes the log line to the server's log buffer and emits an event to registered callbacks.
symbols.limitations: Does not emit any events if the log buffer is full.

symbols.name: emit
symbols.purpose: Emits an event to registered callbacks.
symbols.behavior: Updates the server's status and emits an event to registered callbacks.
symbols.limitations: Does not emit any events if the server is not running.

symbols.name: detectBinary
symbols.purpose: Detects the llama-server binary on the system.
symbols.behavior: Checks a list of possible binary locations and returns the first one that exists.
symbols.limitations: Fails if none of the binary locations are found.

symbols.name: commandExists
symbols.purpose: Checks if a command exists on the system.
symbols.behavior: Executes a command to check if the binary exists and returns a promise with the result.
symbols.limitations: Fails if the command execution fails.
